Mtn To Compel
Off Calendar May-22-2013 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF SAFEWAY INC.'s Motion To Compel is off calendar per moving party. Judge: Richard Kramer; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-14-2013 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Nicholas Napolitan, Esq. of Morrison & Foerster, Tel. 415.268.6789, for defendant and moving party;
William F. Murphy, Esq. of Dillingham & Murphy, Tel. 415.397.2700, for plaintiff and opposing party;
Scott E. Perwin, Esq. of Kenny Nachwalter, Tel. 305.381.7467, for plaintiff and opposing party.

Judge Miller disclosed that she was a partner at Morrison & Foerster from 1997 until February 2005 when she was appointed to the bench. Judge Miller does not know Mr. Napolitan. Judge Miller does not believe that this would affect her ability to be fair and impartial in this case. Court gave counsel the opportunity to ask questions. No questions were asked or objections were raised.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the Court ruled as follows: DEFENDANT MCKESSON CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is overruled in its entirety for reasons stated on the record. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

Mtn To Compel
Off Calendar May-14-2013 Continued To May-22-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

Scott E. Perwin, Kenny Nachwalter, 305.373.1000, appearing for Safeway;
Nicholas Napolitano, Morrison & Foerster LLP, 415.268.6789, appearing for McKesson Corporation.

No stipulation to the Judge Pro Tem by Counsel for Defendant. Matter Argued. PLAINTIFF SAFEWAY INC.'S Motion To Compel is continued to May 22, 2013 at 9:30 am in Department 302. MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter.

At the hearing, Judge Miller disclosed that she was a partner at Morrison & Foerster from 1997 until February 2005 when she was appointed to the bench. Judge Miller occasionally worked on cases with Mr. Bennett. Judge Miller does not believe that this would affect her ability to be fair and impartial in this case.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT MCKESSON CORPORATION's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is continued to April 17, 2013 at 9:30 am in Dept. 302 on the Court's own motion. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
